summary: Lewis Hamilton converted pole position into his maiden Ferrari victory at Barcelona, out-executing George Russell through a strategy battle that unfolded across 66 laps. Hamilton's breakthrough moment came as championship leader Kimi Antonelli's car failed mid-race, removing the primary threat and handing Ferrari a one-two opportunity that Hamilton sealed with precision tire management. The win lifts Hamilton into genuine contention after a turbulent first season with the Scuderia, while Russell's second place keeps Mercedes in the fight despite the strategy setback.
